name: iCal MCP Server
what_it_does: iCal MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with iCal/calendar data — reading calendar events, creating new events, managing recurring meetings, querying free/busy time, and integrating iCalendar scheduling into agent-driven productivity and scheduling workflows on macOS and other platforms supporting iCal.
use_cases: Reading upcoming calendar events from scheduling agents
Creating new calendar events from meeting scheduling agents
Querying free/busy time for scheduling from coordination agents
Managing recurring meetings and reminders from productivity agents
Checking availability for meeting planning from assistant agents
Integrating calendar context into AI-powered productivity workflows
not_for: Google Calendar (use dedicated Google Calendar MCP for that)
Teams using non-iCal calendar systems without iCal sync
Cross-platform calendar management without iCal format support
best_when: A macOS user needs AI agent access to their iCal/Apple Calendar data for scheduling assistance and calendar management.
avoid_when: You primarily use Google Calendar or Outlook — use dedicated MCPs for those services instead.

auth.notes: No authentication — local MCP accessing iCal data via system calendar APIs or ICS files. macOS calendar permissions required.

pricing.notes: Free open source MCP. iCal is standard macOS calendar format.

known_agent_gotchas: macOS calendar permissions required — user must grant access in System Preferences
Community MCP from individual developer — limited ongoing maintenance
iCal data format can be complex (timezones, recurrence rules) — test edge cases
Recurring event handling may have limitations
Calendar modifications are immediate — no dry-run mode
Multiple calendar support (work, personal) requires configuration
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local calendar access. macOS permissions required. No credentials for MCP. Personal schedule data — handle with privacy awareness. Community MCP. No data leaves device.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local calendar data. macOS permissions gate access. No external data exposure. Personal schedule privacy. Community MCP.
